bullets.text: Annual underwriting margin 12.7% — triple the 4% target; Personal Lines CR not stated but 26% NPW growth achieved; 45/48 Agency auto states profitable; all Direct states profitable; crossed $10B written premium milestone in 2003.

NPW $11.9B (+26%); Personal Lines +26%, Commercial Auto +35%; NPW growth of $2.2B in 2002 alone exceeded total premium of 12th largest industry writer; stock appreciated 68.7% in 2003 vs. S&P 500's 28.6%.

Personal Lines PIF grew 57% over past three years; combined policy growth across agency, direct, and commercial at all-time highs; Direct PIF +27% in 2002; improving customer retention propensity confirmed through multi-year analysis.
Invested assets $12.5B (up from $10.3B); shareholders' equity $5.0B; debt-to-capital 23%; 5M shares repurchased at avg $64/share; $200M debt repaid January 2004; ROE 29.1%; comprehensive ROE 35%.

Claims Service Center model expanded to 19 centers in 2003 (12 new); 3,300 new claims representatives hired and trained; over 200 rate and program revisions executed; lowest LAE ratio among agent-distributed competitors.

#3 overall in U.S. personal auto market at 7.0% share (#1 in agent channel at 4.8%); top 15 companies hold 70% market share (up from 54% in 1972); market consolidation accelerating — scale and execution advantages growing.

Internet as primary growth catalyst — Direct channel growing fastest via online sales; agent technology enabling 60%+ of policy changes direct by agents; new claims technology deployment planned for 2004; described as 'technology company in the auto insurance business.'

Concierge Claims Service Centers expanding to 19 locations — redefining vehicle repair as restoring customers to pre-accident condition, not just paying repair costs; improving cycle time, accuracy, and consumer satisfaction simultaneously.

Industry-wide CR expected <100% for 2003 — only second time in 25 years; favorable accident frequency unexpected in extent; severity well in check; industry tailwinds from frequency reduction expected to diminish as competitors reprice and regain profitability.

Underwriting margins expected to move toward planned 4% from current 12.7% as competitive environment normalizes; strong margins provide buffer to focus on retention improvements and faster product innovation introductions.
